violations.rule: Write Scenarios in BDD/Gherkin Form — Scenario/Given/When/Then forces state → action → outcome and surfaces ambiguity while it's cheap to close
violations.why: Enter-key behavior branches on slash-menu-open state and on which modifier key is held, but is described only as prose bullets, never as Given/When/Then scenarios — exactly the structure that would have forced the missing streaming/empty-draft guard to be decided explicitly instead of left implicit.

violations.rule: Handle errors explicitly, never swallow them; every boundary a design introduces needs stated error handling
violations.why: The new Cmd+Enter/Ctrl+Enter path is specified as calling event.preventDefault() then handleSend() on event.metaKey || event.ctrlKey alone, without restating the !isStreaming && draft.trim() guard the current Enter-to-send path enforces (AssistantPanel.tsx:164); as written it could send an empty/whitespace draft or fire while the assistant is streaming.

notes: Sizing values ('~2 rows', '~8 rows') are approximate rather than named constants — low risk, noted not cited.
Testing section leaves resize-logic extraction as conditional ('if extracted...') rather than a firm decision — test-authoring detail, not a behavior gap.
AssistantPanel.tsx already 454 lines (over the ~400-line soft preference) before this change; still well under the 800-line hard cap.
writing-secure-code skill read and judged not applicable — change is UI/input-mechanics only, SSE/model-call boundary and backend explicitly out of scope in the spec.
No diagram included; judged unnecessary under YAGNI for a small single-file, no-new-dependency change, consistent with sibling specs of the same scope.
All line-number and behavior citations in the spec (AssistantPanel.tsx:161-165, :285-292, placeholder text, slashQuery regex, SLASH_ACTIONS fill pattern) verified accurate against the live file.

Round 1's two violations (missing Given/When/Then for §3 Enter-key branching; missing !isStreaming && draft.trim() guard on the Cmd/Ctrl+Enter path) are both resolved: §3 now has four explicit Given/When/Then scenarios, and the fourth restates the guard and its newline-insertion fallback, matched by an updated Testing section.

All line-number and behavior citations in the spec (AssistantPanel.tsx:161-165, :172, :285-292, placeholder text, slashQuery regex, SLASH_ACTIONS fill pattern) re-verified accurate against the live file at this HEAD.

Toolchain (S8) names existing deps at major-version granularity rather than exact pins; acceptable because the spec introduces zero new dependencies and reuses versions already pinned in repo lockfiles.
S4 offers 'drop the underscore or re-export' for _authoritative_events; prefer re-export to honour extend-not-alter for the shared scoring module (scoring.py:119 calls it internally).
S5.1 uses Python round() (banker's rounding); per-queen pick_pct need not sum to 100 — deterministic but worth an implementer comment.

violations.rule: Pin Exact Versions — "an unpinned dependency is a time machine... Pin the exact version of every library and tool"
violations.why: Only @supabase/supabase-js is exact; react/react-dom (^19.2.1), vitest (^4.1.10), @testing-library/react (^16.3.2), @testing-library/user-event (^14.6.1) and typescript (^5.9.3) are caret ranges, and ^19.2.1 has already drifted from the installed tree (package-lock.json resolves React to 19.2.7).

violations.rule: KISS, DRY, YAGNI; prefer the simplest solution that fully solves the problem
violations.why: The mitigation adds a second source of truth for one fact (session.user.new_email ?? pendingEmail) plus a dedicated test, guarding a LOW-rated hypothesis that §4.2 already asserts cannot occur, when the spec's own §10.1 pattern of verifying first would settle it without the redundant branch.

violations.rule: Good, bad, and edge-case scenarios — "anything you leave implicit, the agent infers, and inference is where the defects come from"
violations.why: §4.2 types session.user.email as string | undefined, but §5.1's "Signed in as" line and §7's same-as-current comparison consume it unconditionally, and §8 enumerates only supabase === null and session === null, leaving the undefined-email branch to inference.

notes: §9's CSP row states unconditionally that the Supabase host is already in connect-src; that holds only for the web build (vite.config.web.ts + build/csp.ts rewrite from VITE_SUPABASE_URL). The Electron baseline in src/renderer/index.html is `connect-src 'self' http://localhost:8000` with no Supabase origin. Not a regression (SignInScreen/AuthProvider already operate under it) but broader than what §11.2's dev:web-only manual gate verifies.

§10.2 vs §4.3 wiring is unstated: runAuthAction returns Feedback | null and its AuthResult type discards data, so a literal reading leaves no path to read data.user.new_email. Implementable via the caller's action closure, but never said. Moot if the §10.2 violation is resolved by deletion.
Re-submitting an email change while one is already pending has no scenario in §7; allow/warn/block is left to inference.
The generic throw message 'Something went wrong. Please try again.' is asserted by §6.1 and T-07 but absent from §5.3's copy table, which claims to hold every user-visible string.

DRY on the duplicated run() helper (§4.3) is correctly handled, not a violation: cost quantified, follow-up recorded in §13, flagged as a reviewer-overturnable judgment call per core-conduct's drive-by-cleanup rule.
File-size and naming conventions met: largest new file ~110 lines; PascalCase components, UPPER_SNAKE_CASE message constants, is-prefixed booleans.

writing-secure-code read and in territory (auth + credentials); no violations to cite. No hardcoded secrets, no new model/DB/prompt path, no dangerouslySetInnerHTML, no IDOR surface; the server-issued nonce is a strict improvement over the current sign-out-and-reset path.

Independently verified against the codebase and all held: SignInScreen.tsx:25-41 run() helper, :120-127 feedback roles, :95-99 noValidate rationale, ProfileScreen.tsx:146 session guard and the fieldset/Avatar insertion point, all 11 Tailwind tokens, auth/validation.ts strings, and the nonce/new_email/reauthenticate typings in the installed @supabase/auth-js.
Four user-locked brainstorm decisions (nonce over current-password, neutral email copy, both flows in scope, per-component file layout) treated as approved intent and not re-litigated.
Repo layer: no .claude/project-standards.md exists in mtg-wizard; the repo CLAUDE.md security review checklist was applied and §9 addresses every row.

§3's architecture table still says AccountSection 'returns null in bypass mode' though there are now three null-return conditions (§8.1, §8.2, §8.6); the §8 sections are authoritative and unambiguous, the table summary is just stale.

§7 specifies trimming for the same-as-current comparison but never says whether updateUser({email}) receives the raw or trimmed value; worst case is Supabase rejecting a padded address and rendering the error inline.
No 'updateUser throws' scenario in the email flow (§7); behavior is still fully determined by §4.3's helper contract, so it is an untested path rather than an unspecified one.
§8.2 says ProfileScreen.tsx:146 guards 'the same way', but that line uses disabled={session === null} rather than a null return; the precedent invoked is real, the phrasing is imprecise.

Scope item 4's backlog bookkeeping (moving the shipped assistant-chat-input idea out of ideas.md Unprocessed) belongs to PR #24 rather than this feature; declared in scope and docs-only, so a reviewer preference call rather than a drive-by-cleanup violation.

Verified independently at this HEAD: all 8 §12 versions match apps/desktop/node_modules exactly; npm run test -- --run yields 277 passed across 35 files as §11.1 claims; SignInScreen.test.tsx has 11 tests; index.html CSP baseline has no Supabase origin and build/csp.ts + vite.config.web.ts exist as §9 describes.

Round-1 notes still open and still non-blocking: §5.3's copy table omits the generic throw message 'Something went wrong. Please try again.', and §7 has no scenario for re-submitting an email while a change is already pending.

violations.rule: Pin Exact Versions — "Pin the exact version of every library and tool… double-check any version number the agent proposes against current documentation"
violations.why: The spec names claude, jq, shellcheck, cmux, tmux and pins none; the load-bearing one — the claude CLI whose --bare/--agent flags, hook-payload schema, and PreToolUse matcher name the design depends on — is explicitly deferred ('not re-tested here'), and the installed 2.1.216 in fact diverges from the spec's model of --bare (hooks and CLAUDE.md skipped, auth restricted to ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/apiKeyHelper), exactly the drift pinning-and-verifying would have caught.

violations.rule: "Judgment-based checkpoints that must never be silently skipped"; default-branch/doc/merge/judge gates enforced by Tier-1 hooks
violations.where: Components / run-pane-agent.sh; Error handling table, Recursion row
violations.why: Per the installed CLI, --bare skips hooks and CLAUDE.md auto-discovery, so implementer pane sessions — agents that commit code — run outside git-guard/doc-guard/merge-guard/judge-guard and without core-conduct, a consequence the spec neither states nor mitigates; its own recursion row prescribes CLAUDE_PANE_AGENT=1 to short-circuit hooks inside panes, showing the spec's model assumes pane hooks fire at all.

violations.rule: "API contracts… give the agent the real data structures and interface boundaries to build against, instead of letting it improvise shapes that other components then fail to match"
violations.why: The result file is the design's central interface, yet only its final-line sentinel is specified: the runner invokes claude --output-format json but writes a .md result file, and nothing states whether the body is the raw JSON envelope, the extracted result field, or free text — writer and reader are left to improvise a shape independently.

violations.rule: "Command injection: avoid direct shell execution primitives… Use strongly-typed APIs or safe wrapper libraries"; core-conduct: "Validate all input at system boundaries"
violations.why: open_pane <title> <cwd> <command...> embeds caller-supplied strings into osascript AppleScript source and tmux/cmux command lines with no stated quoting, escaping, or validation rule — the classic AppleScript-interpolation injection surface — and the otherwise thorough zero-trust paragraph covers every boundary except this one.

notes: Auth provisioning for panes unstated: --bare on 2.1.216 authenticates strictly via ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or apiKeyHelper (OAuth/keychain never read); an OAuth-authenticated user would see every pane dispatch fail and silently degrade to in-process forever — folded under pinned-versions rather than cited separately.
FAILED-sentinel reaction readable two ways ('falls back in-process or surfaces to the user') — the dispatching-pane-agents skill should own the choice but the spec should name the default.
Handoff successor environment unspecified: if the press-Enter wrapper leaks CLAUDE_PANE_AGENT into the successor session, the replacement main session permanently loses both new hooks.
'Fail closed on parse errors (guard allows…)' is inverted terminology — allowing is behaviorally fail-open to today's behavior; the decision is right, the label misleads.
Generated stores' permissions unstated (pane-results/, panes/state/); uid-scoped local dirs keep this low-stakes.

Verified and holding: ADR 0007 present; feature/judge-terminal-enforcement undeleted; judge-guard.sh present, contract untouched; statusline orange threshold exactly 75000 (statusline-command.sh:91); --bare and --agent exist on installed CLI 2.1.216; Gherkin lean; canonical spec path; instruction-tier table matches triaging-new-instructions.

Carried: FAILED-sentinel reaction still readable two ways ('falls back in-process or surfaces to the user'); the dispatching-pane-agents skill owns the fallback rules and must name the default when authored.
Carried: handoff wrapper environment unstated; one line ('the handoff launcher does not set CLAUDE_PANE_AGENT') would remove doubt that the successor main session runs with both new hooks live.
Carried: 'fail closed on parse errors (guard allows...)' is inverted terminology — behaviorally fail-open to today's still-guarded behavior; right decision, misleading label.
Partially improved: launchers now mode 700, but pane-results/ and panes/state/ permissions remain unstated; uid-scoped local dirs, low stakes.
New, minor: session-keyed state files (adapter-failed-<session_id>, watcher fired-flags) have no cleanup policy and accumulate in panes/state/; housekeeping only.

Verified this round: all Toolchain pins match installed binaries (claude 2.1.216, jq 1.7.1, tmux 3.6a, cmux 0.64.20 (100); shellcheck absent as stated); --bare absent from the whole document; guard's four-condition deny matrix and error-handling table mutually consistent.

Open questions are verification tasks with named resolution mechanisms (live probe; fixtures), not TBDs — respawn-pane --command quoting must actually be pinned during the probe before the REUSE path is coded
Aux-surface reuse extension is correctly surfaced for user sign-off; this pass does not constitute that sign-off

Path shorthand: 'relative to ~/.claude/' + 'state/runs/…' literally resolves wrong (real dir is panes/state/runs/); harmless since the marker path derives from the prompt file's dirname with a shape guard
PANE_CMUX_BIN absolute-path default carries the pre-existing committed default (cmux.sh:11), improved by the env override — noted against zero-trust, not cited
agent-exit marker holds only DONE/FAILED in existing uid-scoped runs dirs; permissions posture inherited (same carried note as v1)
Assumption 3 (restore-session title survival) deliberately observational with a benign degrade path

Verified: named suites/scripts exist; live run-ids match the grammar; PANE_STATE_DIR/PANE_CLAUDE_BIN precedents real; title grammar fits the frozen allowlist; pins match same-day binary verification from the v1 round-2 writeup (binaries not re-run)
